label: Ask a question
description: Ask questions to collect needed information from users in a clear, minimal, and respectful way.
purpose: Collect the minimum information needed from the user to make a decision, complete a transaction, or personalise the service.
use_when: A service needs information directly from the user to make a decision or complete a transaction.
Information cannot be obtained from integrations or matched from existing systems.
do_not_use_when: The information is already available from existing government systems or integrations.
The data can be pre-filled and only needs user confirmation.
Asking the question does not add value to the service outcome.
The request is optional or 'nice to have' rather than necessary.

outputs.label: User answer(s)
outputs.description: The data provided by the user in response to the question(s).

variants.label: Question group
variants.description: Collect related information together (e.g. contact details) under a shared heading.
use_when: Multiple related fields belong together and grouping aids user understanding.

variants.label: Branching question
variants.description: Follow-up questions appear only when relevant to prior answers. Users see only the questions they need to answer.
use_when: Answers to some questions determine which questions are shown next.

ux_considerations.text: Ask one thing at a time. Ask for information only when truly needed.
Use the correct field type: text, number, date, radio, checkbox, dropdown, or file upload.
Error messages must explain what is wrong and how to fix it.
Mark optional questions explicitly with '(optional)'. Do not use asterisks for mandatory fields.
Enable save and return for complex or sensitive question sets.

technical_notes.text: Authentication is required to protect saved answers if save-and-return is enabled.
Answers must be validated server-side before being accepted as outputs.
